name: Functional Role Taxonomy
description: Controlled project-independent Card Knowledge vocabulary for describing what cards can do in Commander deck construction terms.

includes: Role labels, categories, descriptions, typical signals, and notes
Vocabulary suitable for Commander analysis, including artifact and combo decks

categories.label: Mana Development
categories.description: Roles that increase, accelerate, discount, fix, or otherwise improve access to mana.

categories.label: Card Advantage
categories.description: Roles that increase access to cards through draw, temporary access, or repeatable resource flow.

categories.label: Selection and Tutoring
categories.description: Roles that search, filter, order, or otherwise improve the quality and specificity of card access.

categories.label: Interaction
categories.description: Roles that answer opposing cards, strategies, or game actions.

categories.label: Protection and Resilience
categories.description: Roles that help cards, boards, or game plans survive disruption.

categories.label: Recursion and Recovery
categories.description: Roles that return cards or resources from graveyards, exile, or other non-battlefield zones.

categories.label: Engine and Scaling
categories.description: Roles that create repeatable value loops, amplify resources, or scale with board and game state.

categories.label: Combo and Win Conditions
categories.description: Roles that participate in combo lines, convert resources into wins, or close games.

categories.label: Board Presence
categories.description: Roles that add bodies, blockers, attackers, or board pressure.

categories.label: Utility and Support
categories.description: Roles that enable plans, modify rules, or provide flexible non-primary support functions.

categories.label: Lands and Mana Base
categories.description: Roles specific to land-based color access, utility, fixing, and mana base structure.

roles.label: Ramp
roles.description: Increases the amount of mana available in future turns beyond normal land drops.
roles.notes: General acceleration role; more specific mana roles may also apply later.

roles.label: Mana Rock
roles.description: Artifact-based mana source that can be used for acceleration or fixing.
typical_signals: Converts mana into one or more colors
roles.notes: Especially important for artifact decks and colorless-heavy Commander decks.

roles.label: Cost Reduction
roles.description: Reduces costs to cast spells or activate abilities.
typical_signals: Activated abilities cost less to activate
Discount applies to a card type, color, or strategy subset
roles.notes: Tracks mana efficiency rather than mana production.

roles.label: Ritual or Burst Mana
roles.description: Provides a temporary increase in mana or resources, usually for a single turn or spell sequence.
typical_signals: Sacrifices or consumes a resource for mana
roles.notes: Useful for combo turns and explosive setup but not persistent ramp.

roles.label: Mana Fixing
roles.description: Improves access to required colors or converts mana into more useful colors.
typical_signals: Filters one mana into another color
Chooses colors or supports multicolor casting
roles.notes: May overlap with mana rocks or fixing lands once card assignments exist.

roles.label: Land Ramp
roles.description: Accelerates mana by finding or placing lands onto the battlefield.
roles.notes: Distinct from land tutoring when the land directly increases battlefield mana.

roles.label: Utility Land Mana
roles.description: Land-based mana contribution with additional utility beyond basic color production.
typical_signals: Land produces mana and has non-mana abilities
Land produces conditional or enhanced mana
Land supports a specific card type or strategy
roles.notes: Bridges mana development and mana base utility.

roles.label: Repeatable Card Draw
roles.description: Provides ongoing access to additional cards over multiple turns or repeated triggers.
typical_signals: Has an activated ability that draws cards
Creates recurring card access from a board state
roles.notes: Represents sustained resource flow, not one-time draw.

roles.label: Burst Card Draw
roles.description: Provides a one-time increase in cards drawn or cards put into hand.
roles.notes: May be efficient even when not repeatable.

roles.label: Impulse or Selection
roles.description: Provides temporary or constrained access to cards, often from the top of the library or exile.
typical_signals: Exiles cards that may be played for a limited time
Looks at cards and allows one or more to be used
Provides card access with timing or type restrictions
roles.notes: Counts as access to cards even when it does not put cards into hand.

roles.label: Graveyard Card Access
roles.description: Allows cards in graveyards to be played, cast, or otherwise used as available resources.
typical_signals: Plays lands or spells from a graveyard
Treats the graveyard as an extension of available cards
roles.notes: Differs from recursion when the card is used directly from the graveyard rather than returned.

roles.label: Tutor
roles.description: Searches a library for a card or a broad class of cards.
typical_signals: Finds a card matching broad criteria
Puts the found card into hand, graveyard, battlefield, or on top of library
roles.notes: Generic tutor role; more specific tutor roles may also apply.

roles.label: Artifact Tutor
roles.description: Searches for artifacts or cards that specifically support artifact access.
typical_signals: Searches library for an artifact card
Finds an artifact by mana value or subtype
Places an artifact into hand, graveyard, battlefield, or on top of library
roles.notes: Important for artifact and combo decks.

roles.label: Land Tutor
roles.description: Searches for land cards without necessarily accelerating battlefield mana.
typical_signals: Finds a land with specific characteristics
Puts a land into hand or on top of library
roles.notes: Use land_ramp when the primary function is acceleration onto the battlefield.

roles.label: Topdeck Manipulation
roles.description: Changes, orders, reveals, or improves the top cards of the library.
typical_signals: Looks at the top cards of a library
Reorders cards on top of a library
Places known cards on top of a library
roles.notes: Supports draw quality, combo setup, and future card access.

roles.label: Card Filtering
roles.description: Improves hand or draw quality by exchanging, discarding, surveilling, scrying, or selecting among cards.
typical_signals: Scry, surveil, loot, or rummage effects
Draw then discard or discard then draw
Selects a subset of seen cards
roles.notes: Selection quality role; not always net card advantage.

roles.label: Counterspell
roles.description: Counters spells, abilities, or specific game actions on the stack.
roles.notes: Includes conditional and narrow counters.

roles.label: Targeted Removal
roles.description: Removes, neutralizes, or answers one or a small number of specific permanents.
typical_signals: Neutralizes a target with an aura, counter, or similar effect
roles.notes: Does not imply broad sweeper functionality.

roles.label: Board Wipe
roles.description: Removes or resets many permanents at once across one or more card types.
typical_signals: Destroys all permanents of a type
Applies a sweeping negative effect to the battlefield
roles.notes: May be asymmetrical or conditional.

roles.label: Bounce
roles.description: Returns spells or permanents to a player's hand or library as temporary interaction.
typical_signals: Places a spell or permanent back into a library
roles.notes: Can also protect own permanents, but this role tracks interactive tempo.

roles.label: Stax or Hate Piece
roles.description: Constrains, taxes, prevents, or punishes opposing game actions or resource use.
typical_signals: Players cannot perform a game action
Specific strategies are restricted or punished
roles.notes: Role describes functional disruption, not a recommendation to include it.

roles.label: Graveyard Hate
roles.description: Disrupts graveyard use by exiling cards, limiting graveyard access, or preventing recursion.
roles.notes: May be narrow, repeatable, or one-shot.

roles.label: Protection
roles.description: Broadly protects cards, permanents, players, or game plans from opposing interaction.
typical_signals: Protects a board state or key permanent
roles.notes: Generic protection role; more specific protection roles may also apply.

roles.label: Hexproof or Shroud
roles.description: Prevents targeting through hexproof, shroud, ward-like targeting tax, or similar effects.
roles.notes: Tracks targeting protection specifically.

roles.label: Indestructible or Regeneration
roles.description: Helps permanents survive destruction through indestructible, regeneration, or similar replacement effects.
typical_signals: Prevents destruction from lethal damage or destroy effects
roles.notes: Does not cover exile, sacrifice, or bounce resilience unless separately supported.

roles.label: Defensive Pillowfort
roles.description: Discourages or prevents attacks and damage against the player or planeswalkers.
roles.notes: A defensive role rather than direct removal.

roles.label: Anti-Wipe Protection
roles.description: Protects a board from sweepers or enables recovery immediately around mass removal.
typical_signals: Returns a board after a wipe
roles.notes: Specific resilience role for preserving developed boards.

roles.label: Artifact Recursion
roles.description: Returns artifact cards from graveyard, exile, or other zones to hand, battlefield, or library.
roles.notes: Important for artifact decks and reusable combo pieces.

roles.label: Creature Recursion
roles.description: Returns creature cards from graveyard, exile, or other zones to hand, battlefield, or library.
roles.notes: Specific to creature recovery.

roles.label: Graveyard Recursion
roles.description: Returns cards from the graveyard to useful zones without being limited to one permanent type.
typical_signals: Recasts or reuses cards from graveyard
Moves graveyard resources back to hand, battlefield, or library
roles.notes: Broad graveyard recovery role.

roles.label: Permanent Recursion
roles.description: Returns permanent cards or permanents of several types from non-battlefield zones.
typical_signals: Returns artifacts, creatures, enchantments, lands, or planeswalkers
roles.notes: Useful when recursion covers several permanent types.

roles.label: Token Engine
roles.description: Creates tokens repeatedly or as part of a scalable value loop.
typical_signals: Scales token output with game actions
roles.notes: Represents repeatable production rather than a single token effect.

roles.label: Artifact Engine
roles.description: Generates repeatable value from artifacts entering, leaving, tapping, sacrificing, or being controlled.
typical_signals: Scales with artifact count or artifact actions
roles.notes: Core vocabulary for artifact-centric Commander decks.

roles.label: Tap-Untap Engine
roles.description: Creates value by tapping, untapping, or repeatedly reusing permanents.
roles.notes: Can support mana engines, combo lines, and board scaling.

roles.label: Mana Engine
roles.description: Creates repeatable or scaling mana production beyond ordinary ramp.
typical_signals: Produces mana based on board state
Loops mana production with other permanents
roles.notes: Engine role for sustained or scalable mana generation.

roles.label: Payoff Engine
roles.description: Provides repeatable rewards when the deck performs its intended actions.
typical_signals: Triggers on casts, enters, deaths, or activations
Converts repeated actions into cards, tokens, damage, or mana
roles.notes: Describes repeatable payoff infrastructure, not final win status.

roles.label: Scaling Threat
roles.description: Becomes more threatening as resources, permanents, counters, or actions accumulate.
typical_signals: Threat level increases with artifacts, tokens, mana, or spells
roles.notes: May also be a finisher if it commonly closes games.

roles.label: Combo Piece
roles.description: A required or near-required component of a deterministic or repeatable combo line.
typical_signals: Combines with specific effects to produce a loop
Is necessary for a defined combo sequence
roles.notes: This taxonomy only defines the role; it does not map combo relationships.

roles.label: Combo Enabler
roles.description: Makes combo lines easier to start, protect, repeat, or assemble without necessarily being the payoff.
typical_signals: Provides untaps, cost changes, or access needed for loops
Allows combo pieces to be reused
roles.notes: Can overlap with tutors, protection, or engines.

roles.label: Combo Payoff
roles.description: Converts a combo loop or assembled engine into a decisive resource advantage or win.
typical_signals: Turns a loop into damage, cards, tokens, or mana
Wins or nearly wins when repeated
Rewards the repeated action central to a combo
roles.notes: Does not imply the card is useful only in combo contexts.

roles.label: Alternate Win Condition
roles.description: Provides a way to win the game through explicit noncombat or nonstandard victory conditions.
typical_signals: Says a player wins the game
Creates a specific victory condition outside normal damage
roles.notes: Specific to explicit alternate win or loss conditions.

roles.label: Finisher
roles.description: Closes games through combat, damage, overwhelming board impact, or decisive resource conversion.
typical_signals: Can deal lethal or near-lethal damage
Converts developed resources into a game-ending state
roles.notes: Describes endgame function, not a recommendation.

roles.label: Damage Payoff
roles.description: Turns deck actions, board state, or resource loops into damage or life loss.
typical_signals: Causes life loss from repeated actions
Scales damage with artifacts, tokens, spells, or sacrifices
roles.notes: May be a combo payoff or general pressure tool.

roles.label: Creature Body
roles.description: Provides a creature body for attacking, blocking, carrying equipment, or enabling creature-count effects.
typical_signals: Contributes power and toughness to the battlefield
roles.notes: Basic board presence role; does not imply strategic importance.

roles.label: Token Producer
roles.description: Creates one or more tokens as a direct effect.
typical_signals: Creates artifact, treasure, clue, food, or other tokens
Adds multiple objects to the battlefield
roles.notes: Use token_engine for repeatable or scaling token production.

roles.label: Evasive Threat
roles.description: Applies combat pressure through evasion or difficult blocking patterns.
typical_signals: Flying, menace, trample, unblockable, or similar evasion
roles.notes: Combat role only; does not imply the card is a finisher.

roles.label: Blocker
roles.description: Provides defensive combat presence that can absorb, deter, or trade with attacks.
typical_signals: Defensive keywords such as reach or vigilance
roles.notes: Defensive board role.

roles.label: Commander Support
roles.description: Supports the commander through protection, enabling attacks, cost support, or commander-specific game actions.
typical_signals: Enhances commander combat or activated abilities
roles.notes: Project-independent role; project-specific commander weighting belongs elsewhere.

roles.label: Utility Piece
roles.description: Provides flexible support that does not fit cleanly into a more specific primary role.
roles.notes: Use more specific roles where possible during later assignment work.

roles.label: Rule Modifier
roles.description: Changes how game rules, timing, casting, combat, or resource use work.
typical_signals: Modifies hand size, draw rules, combat rules, or spell restrictions
roles.notes: Neutral functional role; may help or hinder depending on deck context.

roles.label: Sacrifice Outlet
roles.description: Allows the controller to sacrifice permanents intentionally for cost, value, or combo purposes.
typical_signals: Sacrifice a permanent as a cost
Sacrifice artifacts or creatures for an effect
roles.notes: Often supports recursion, death triggers, artifact engines, or combo lines.

roles.label: Enabler
roles.description: Makes another plan or class of cards function more reliably without being the payoff itself.
typical_signals: Changes timing or access for other cards
Reduces setup friction for a strategy
roles.notes: Generic support role for enabling deck mechanics.

roles.label: Setup Piece
roles.description: Prepares future turns, engines, or combo sequences without immediately generating the main payoff.
typical_signals: Sets up future card access or board state
roles.notes: Describes preparatory function, not card quality.

roles.label: Colored Source
roles.description: Land or mana-base element that provides colored mana.
typical_signals: Produces one or more colors of mana
roles.notes: Mana base role for color access.

roles.label: Colorless Source
roles.description: Land or mana-base element that provides colorless mana.
roles.notes: Important where colorless costs or artifact synergies matter.

roles.label: Utility Land
roles.description: Land that provides meaningful non-mana function in addition to or instead of mana production.
typical_signals: Provides card selection, recursion, protection, tokens, or interaction
Supports a specific deck function from the land slot
roles.notes: Describes land-slot utility, not project-specific value.

roles.label: Artifact Land
roles.description: Land that is also an artifact or otherwise contributes directly to artifact count and artifact synergies.
typical_signals: Card type includes artifact and land
Counts as an artifact from the mana base
roles.notes: Important for artifact decks because it bridges mana base and artifact density.

roles.label: Fetch or Search Land
roles.description: Land that searches for, finds, or converts itself into another land.
typical_signals: Sacrifices to search for a land
Finds a basic or typed land
Improves mana base access through land search
roles.notes: May support fixing, shuffling, landfall, or graveyard count in later analysis.

roles.label: Fixing Land
roles.description: Land that improves color access or converts mana into needed colors.
typical_signals: Enters with choices that determine color access
roles.notes: Mana base-specific color fixing role.
